ai_disclosure: Drafted and copy-reviewed by AI (Gemini) against the saudade editorial rules. Written for this edition's readers — not translated from English. No human rewrite.

items.headline: The humid haze settles over the Han River at dusk.
items.lede: Office lights reflect on the dark water as the city breathes in the heavy summer air.
items.body: The river parks remain crowded well past midnight. People seek the breeze near the water edge.

items.headline: Lotus flowers bloom in the secret ponds of Gyeongbokgung.
items.lede: The pale petals unfold against the dark, waxy leaves in the stagnant mid-July heat.
items.body: Visitors pause on the wooden bridges to watch the koi. The stone walls provide a brief, cooling shade.

items.headline: Cicadas begin their steady rhythm in the residential alleys.
items.lede: The sound rises from the overgrown gardens tucked behind low brick walls in Samcheong-dong.
items.body: It signals the peak of the season. The streets grow quiet as the sun hits its highest point.

items.headline: Wind chimes hang from the eaves of old Yanaka houses.
items.lede: The glass bells ring softly whenever a breeze drifts through the narrow, shaded corridors.
items.body: Residents leave their doors open to invite the draft. The sound creates a respite from the humidity.

items.headline: Hydrangeas fade to muted green in the temple gardens.
items.lede: The vibrant blue and purple shades of early summer surrender to the relentless sun.
items.body: Gardeners begin the seasonal pruning of the shrubs. The moss remains damp in the deep shadows.

items.headline: The evening trains carry a scent of damp pavement.
items.lede: Brief afternoon showers leave the stations slick and cool before the night commute begins.
items.body: Commuters fold their umbrellas with practiced precision. The platform air feels heavy and still.

items.headline: The Tagus reflects a blinding, silver light at noon.
items.lede: The river remains calm while the city climbs the steep hills under a cloudless sky.
items.body: Fishermen retreat to the shade of the stone piers. The boats bob rhythmically in the tide.

items.headline: Bougainvillea spills over the white walls of Alfama.
items.lede: Bright pink blossoms cling to the sun-baked stone, softening the sharp edges of the ancient quarter.
items.body: Narrow alleys trap the heat until late in the evening. Residents wait for the Atlantic wind.

items.headline: Evening meals move to the sidewalk tables.
items.lede: The air finally softens as the sun dips behind the western hills of the city.
items.body: Clinking glasses replace the noise of afternoon traffic. The scent of grilled fish lingers in the breeze.
